meaning: A file sent along with an email message
example: Please find the report as an attachment to this email.

meaning: Carbon Copy; sending a copy of an email to additional recipients
example: I will CC the manager so she is aware of our discussion.

meaning: Blind Carbon Copy; sending a copy to recipients without others knowing
example: He BCCed the HR department on the sensitive message.

meaning: Responding to an email so that all original recipients receive the response
example: Do not reply-all unless your response is relevant to everyone.

meaning: The brief heading of an email that summarises its content
example: A clear subject line helps recipients prioritise their inbox.

meaning: The folder where incoming messages are received
example: She checks her inbox first thing every morning.

meaning: Unsolicited or irrelevant messages sent in bulk, especially by email
example: The promotional newsletter ended up in his spam folder.

meaning: A chain of email messages on the same topic
example: Scroll up the thread to read the full conversation history.

meaning: An automatic response sent when someone is unavailable
example: She set an auto-reply message while on vacation.

meaning: A block of text appended to the end of an email with the sender's details
example: His email signature includes his phone number and job title.

meaning: To send a received email to another person
example: Please forward the invitation to your colleague.

meaning: An unsent, saved version of a message
example: She saved the email as a draft before finalising it.

meaning: A fraudulent attempt to obtain sensitive information via deceptive emails
example: The phishing email appeared to come from the bank but was fake.

meaning: A collection of email addresses used to send messages to multiple people at once
example: Subscribe to our mailing list for weekly updates.

meaning: A regularly distributed email containing news and updates
example: The company sends a newsletter to all clients every Friday.

meaning: A real-time communication session that transmits both audio and video
example: They held a video call to discuss the project milestones.

meaning: A meeting conducted online using video or audio conferencing tools
example: The team scheduled a virtual meeting for Monday morning.

meaning: To silence one's microphone during a call so others cannot hear
example: Please mute yourself when you are not speaking to reduce background noise.

meaning: To re-enable the microphone so others can hear the speaker
example: You can unmute now and share your thoughts.

meaning: Displaying one's computer screen to other participants in a call
example: She used screen share to walk the team through the slides.

meaning: A smaller virtual room created within a larger video meeting for group discussions
example: Participants were divided into breakout rooms for the workshop activity.

meaning: The data transmission capacity of a network connection
example: Low bandwidth caused the video to freeze during the call.

meaning: The delay between sending and receiving data in a communication system
example: High latency made the conversation difficult as responses were delayed.

meaning: A digitally applied image or video shown behind a person on a video call
example: He used a blurred virtual background to hide his messy room.

meaning: An online seminar or presentation conducted via the internet
example: Over 500 people attended the live webinar on digital marketing.

meaning: The person who organises and manages a video call or online event
example: The host can remove disruptive participants from the meeting.

meaning: A virtual holding area where participants wait before being admitted to a meeting
example: Attendees stayed in the waiting room until the host started the session.

meaning: A saved audio or video file of a meeting or call
example: A recording of the webinar was sent to those who missed it.

meaning: Live or auto-generated text displayed during a video call showing spoken words
example: She enabled captions so the hearing-impaired participant could follow along.

meaning: A short text message sent and received in real time over the internet
example: She sent an instant message to ask if he was free to talk.

meaning: A private message sent to a specific individual on a platform
example: Send me a direct message if you have any questions.

meaning: A messaging conversation involving three or more participants
example: The project team uses a group chat for quick daily updates.

meaning: A small digital image or icon used to express emotion in text
example: She added a thumbs-up emoji to show her agreement.

meaning: A short, looping animated image used in digital communication
example: He replied to the good news with a celebratory GIF.

meaning: A larger, illustrated digital image used like an emoji in messaging apps
example: The app offers custom sticker packs for expressive chats.

meaning: A notification indicating that the recipient has seen a message
example: The blue ticks serve as read receipts in many messaging apps.

meaning: An alert informing a user of a new message or event
example: He turned off notifications during the meeting to avoid distractions.

meaning: To highlight and fix an important message at the top of a chat
example: The manager pinned the meeting agenda in the team chat.

meaning: Tagging a specific person in a message using @ to alert them
example: Use @John in the chat to make sure he sees the update.

meaning: An emoji-based response to a specific message without typing a reply
example: He gave a heart reaction to show appreciation for the update.

meaning: A series of replies nested under a specific message in a chat
example: Reply in the thread to keep the main channel uncluttered.

meaning: A status setting that suppresses incoming message notifications
example: She activated do not disturb during focused work hours.

meaning: An automated program that simulates conversation with users
example: The chatbot answered basic customer queries instantly.

meaning: A message that is encoded so only the intended recipient can read it
example: The app sends encrypted messages to protect user privacy.

meaning: A set of slides used in a presentation, typically created with software
example: She prepared a polished slide deck for the investor pitch.

meaning: A display mode showing the speaker's notes and upcoming slides during a presentation
example: He used presenter view so he could see his notes while the audience saw the slides.

meaning: A short piece of information highlighted with a dot or symbol in a list
example: Keep bullet points concise for better audience readability.

meaning: A visual representation of information or data using graphics
example: The infographic summarised the survey results in a clear format.

meaning: An animated effect between slides in a presentation
example: Avoid overusing flashy transitions as they can distract the audience.

meaning: Movement effects applied to text or objects within a presentation slide
example: Each bullet point appeared with a fade animation.

meaning: A segment of a presentation where the audience asks questions
example: Please save your questions for the Q&A session at the end.

meaning: A printed or digital document distributed to audience members during a presentation
example: Participants received a handout summarising the key points.

meaning: A persuasive presentation aimed at selling an idea or product
example: The startup delivered a compelling pitch to attract investors.

meaning: The graphical representation of information and data
example: Effective data visualisation makes complex trends easy to understand.

meaning: A digital or physical surface used to draw and write ideas during a presentation
example: The trainer used the interactive whiteboard to illustrate the concept.

meaning: A detailed timeline of events during a presentation or event
example: The event coordinator shared the run of show with all speakers.

meaning: Key ideas or facts a speaker wishes to convey during a presentation
example: He prepared talking points to stay on track during the interview.

meaning: An instruction prompting the audience to take a specific next step
example: End your presentation with a clear call to action.

meaning: Working from a location outside the traditional office, often from home
example: Remote work has become the norm for many tech companies.

meaning: Communication that does not require all parties to be available at the same time
example: Asynchronous communication allows global teams to collaborate across time zones.

meaning: Communication that occurs in real time with all parties present simultaneously
example: Video calls are a form of synchronous communication.

meaning: Online storage of data accessible from any device with internet access
example: The team stores all project files on cloud storage for easy access.

meaning: A file that multiple users can view and edit simultaneously online
example: They used a shared document to co-write the project report.

meaning: A system that tracks changes to files so previous versions can be restored
example: Version control ensures no edits are permanently lost.

meaning: Software designed to help teams work together efficiently online
example: Slack and Trello are popular collaboration tools for remote teams.

meaning: A virtual environment where employees access work tools and communicate online
example: The company set up a digital workspace for its distributed team.

meaning: A brief daily check-in meeting where team members share progress and blockers
example: The team holds a 15-minute stand-up meeting every morning.

meaning: The process of tracking, organising, and prioritising work tasks
example: Effective task management keeps the remote team aligned on priorities.

meaning: The latest time by which a task or project must be completed
example: All team members must submit their sections before the deadline.

meaning: A key point or significant achievement in a project's timeline
example: Completing the prototype was an important project milestone.

meaning: A defined sequence of steps or processes followed to complete a task
example: An efficient workflow reduces time wasted on repetitive steps.

meaning: A project management approach based on iterative progress and flexibility
example: The development team follows an agile methodology with two-week sprints.

meaning: A short, time-boxed period in which a team completes a set amount of work
example: The team plans new features at the start of each sprint.

meaning: Virtual Private Network; a secure connection to a private network over the internet
example: Employees connect via VPN to access internal company systems remotely.

meaning: A region of the globe observing a uniform standard time
example: Schedule meetings mindful of everyone's time zone.

meaning: The times during which a person is reachable and able to communicate
example: Share your availability on the shared calendar so the team can plan meetings.

meaning: A process where responses to actions are used to improve future performance
example: Regular check-ins create a healthy feedback loop within the team.

meaning: A work model combining in-office and remote working
example: The company adopted a hybrid work policy allowing two remote days per week.

meaning: To send a brief message to get someone's attention quickly
example: Ping me on Slack when the document is ready to review.

meaning: A telephone or online call involving multiple participants simultaneously
example: All regional managers joined the conference call to hear the quarterly results.

meaning: Joining a call by telephone rather than through an app
example: Participants without internet access can use the dial-in number.

meaning: A message sent after a meeting or conversation to summarise actions or next steps
example: She sent a follow-up email recapping the decisions made in the meeting.

meaning: An automatic email reply informing senders that the recipient is away
example: He set an out-of-office message for the duration of his leave.

meaning: To remove oneself from an email mailing list
example: Click the link at the bottom of the email to unsubscribe.

meaning: To mark an email as important for easy retrieval later
example: She flagged the email from the client to address it later.

meaning: To move emails out of the inbox into a storage folder without deleting them
example: Archive old emails to keep your inbox organised.

meaning: A tag applied to an email to categorise and organise messages
example: She uses labels like 'urgent' and 'invoices' to sort her inbox.

meaning: To insert content such as an image, video, or link directly into a message
example: He embedded the video link in the email so recipients could watch immediately.

meaning: A clickable link in a digital message that redirects to a webpage or file
example: Click the hyperlink in the email to access the registration form.

meaning: The ability of an email to successfully reach the recipient's inbox
example: Poor email deliverability results in messages being marked as spam.

meaning: An interactive feature in a message or meeting allowing participants to vote on options
example: The host launched a live poll to gauge audience opinions.

meaning: The person delivering a presentation or leading a meeting
example: The presenter spoke clearly and maintained eye contact with the camera.

meaning: Techniques used to involve and interest the audience during a presentation
example: Quizzes and polls improve audience engagement during webinars.

meaning: The most important point or lesson from a presentation or discussion
example: The key takeaway is that early planning reduces project risks.

meaning: A list of topics or items to be discussed in a meeting
example: Please review the agenda before the meeting begins.

meaning: A written record of what was discussed and decided in a meeting
example: The secretary shared the meeting minutes with all participants.

meaning: A specific task assigned to a person following a meeting or discussion
example: Each action item was assigned an owner and a due date.

meaning: A brief meeting or conversation to align team members on status or decisions
example: Let's have a quick sync before the client call.

meaning: The process of integrating a new employee or user into a system or organisation
example: Virtual onboarding helps remote employees feel welcomed from day one.

meaning: A centralised repository of information and documentation for a team or organisation
example: New employees can find answers in the company knowledge base.

meaning: To refer a problem to a higher authority or more experienced person
example: If the issue is not resolved today, we will need to escalate it.

meaning: A person or group with an interest in the outcome of a project or decision
example: Keep all stakeholders informed of project progress via weekly updates.

meaning: A point of contact or interaction between team members or with a client
example: Regular touchpoints help maintain strong client relationships.

meaning: A person's capacity to take on more work or responsibilities
example: I don't have the bandwidth to take on another project this week.

meaning: An obstacle preventing progress on a task or project
example: The delayed approval is a blocker for the entire team.

meaning: A meeting held after a project or sprint to reflect on what went well and what to improve
example: The team's retrospective revealed a need for clearer communication.

meaning: Choosing to receive messages or communications voluntarily
example: Users must opt-in to receive marketing emails.

meaning: A message sent to many recipients at once, typically one-way
example: The company sent a broadcast message to announce the new policy.

meaning: A brief message communicating current progress or situation
example: She posted a status update in the project channel every Friday.

meaning: Written materials that record processes, decisions, or information
example: Good documentation allows team members to work independently.

meaning: Involving members from different departments or specialisations
example: The cross-functional team included developers, designers, and marketers.

meaning: The connection of different software tools so they work together seamlessly
example: The Slack and Google Calendar integration sends meeting reminders automatically.

meaning: A visual interface displaying key information and metrics at a glance
example: The project dashboard shows task completion rates in real time.

meaning: A note or comment added to a document or presentation slide
example: She added annotations to highlight key data points on the slide.

meaning: A pre-designed format or structure used to create documents or messages
example: Use the email template for consistent brand communication.

meaning: A brief reply confirming receipt of a message or information
example: Please send an acknowledgement once you receive the brief.

meaning: Working together on shared content simultaneously over the internet
example: Real-time collaboration tools allow multiple editors to update a document at once.

meaning: To transfer a file from a local device to the internet or a shared system
example: Please upload the final version of the report to the shared drive.

meaning: To transfer a file from the internet or a shared system to a local device
example: Download the meeting recording from the link in your email.

meaning: Access rights granted to a user to view or edit a file or system
example: You need edit permission to make changes to the shared spreadsheet.

meaning: A person who shares the role of presenting alongside the main presenter
example: The marketing director joined as co-presenter for the product launch webinar.

meaning: A person who manages the flow of a meeting, webinar, or online discussion
example: The moderator collected audience questions during the live session.

meaning: A feature that highlights one participant's video above others in a call
example: The host used spotlight to focus the audience's attention on the speaker.

meaning: A dedicated space in a messaging platform for a specific topic or team
example: Post design-related updates in the #design channel.

meaning: An organisation's dedicated area within a collaboration or messaging platform
example: All company communications happen in our Slack workspace.

meaning: A URL shared with participants to join a meeting or platform
example: Copy the invite link and send it to all confirmed attendees.

meaning: A security code required to enter a virtual meeting or account
example: Enter the passcode shown in the meeting invitation to join the call.

meaning: A written record of spoken words from a meeting or call
example: AI-generated transcription makes it easy to review what was said in the meeting.

meaning: A virtual signal used in online meetings to indicate a desire to speak
example: Use the raise hand feature to ask a question without interrupting the speaker.

meaning: An image representing a user on a digital platform or messaging app
example: Use a professional profile picture for work-related platforms.

meaning: A username or identifier used on a digital platform
example: Her Twitter handle is @sarahdesigns.

meaning: Linking a person or topic in a post or message to draw attention
example: She posted the update and tagged the relevant team members.

meaning: A status message informing others that a user is temporarily unavailable
example: His away message said he would be back in an hour.

meaning: Software or services hosted on remote internet servers rather than locally
example: The company switched to a cloud-based communication platform.

meaning: A security method ensuring only communicating users can read the messages
example: The app uses end-to-end encryption to protect private conversations.
